persons.meaning_connection: Formed by God from the dust of the ground (adamah), the first man created in God's image.
persons.life_summary: The first man, created by God in His image from the dust of the ground. He was placed in the Garden of Eden to tend it and given dominion over all living creatures. After disobeying God by eating from the tree of knowledge, he was expelled from Eden.

persons.meaning_connection: Named by Adam because she was the mother of all living (chai).
persons.life_summary: The first woman, formed by God from Adam's rib as a companion and helper. She was deceived by the serpent and ate from the forbidden tree, then gave the fruit to Adam. She bore Cain, Abel, and Seth, and was called the mother of all living.

persons.meaning_connection: Eve said 'I have acquired a man with the help of the LORD' at his birth.
persons.life_summary: The firstborn son of Adam and Eve, a tiller of the ground. He murdered his brother Abel out of jealousy when God accepted Abel's offering but not his. God cursed him to be a wanderer, and he settled in the land of Nod east of Eden.

persons.meaning_connection: His name foreshadowed his brief life—like a breath or vapor, he was gone too soon, slain by his brother.
persons.life_summary: The second son of Adam and Eve, a keeper of sheep. He offered the firstborn of his flock to God, which was accepted. He was murdered by his brother Cain and is remembered as the first righteous martyr.

persons.meaning_connection: Eve named him Seth saying 'God has appointed me another seed instead of Abel, whom Cain killed.'
persons.life_summary: The third named son of Adam and Eve, born after Abel's murder. Through his line, the godly lineage continued from Adam to Noah. He lived 912 years and fathered Enosh.

persons.meaning_connection: His name reflects the frailty and mortality of humankind. In his time, people began to call upon the name of the LORD.
persons.life_summary: Son of Seth and grandson of Adam. In his days, people began to call upon the name of the LORD, marking a renewal of worship. He lived 905 years.

persons.meaning_connection: His name suggests acquisition or craftsmanship, continuing the faithful line from Seth through which God's promise would be carried.
persons.life_summary: Son of Enosh and great-grandson of Adam. He continued the godly line of Seth, living 910 years and fathering Mahalalel.

persons.meaning_connection: His name is a declaration of worship—'the praise of God'—reflecting the continuing faith of Seth's line.
persons.life_summary: Son of Kenan, his name means 'praise of God,' reflecting the devotion of the Sethite line. He lived 895 years and fathered Jared.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'descent,' possibly indicating a spiritual decline in the world during his time, or simply a going down from the highlands.
persons.life_summary: Son of Mahalalel and father of Enoch. He lived 962 years, the second-longest lifespan recorded in Scripture after Methuselah.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'dedicated,' and he lived a life wholly dedicated to God—walking with God so closely that God took him without death.
persons.life_summary: Son of Jared and father of Methuselah. He walked with God for 365 years and did not see death, for God took him. He is one of only two people in Scripture who did not die. He prophesied about the coming judgment.

persons.name_meaning: man of the dart, or 'when he dies, it shall come'
persons.meaning_connection: Tradition holds his name prophetically means 'when he dies, it shall be sent'—and indeed the Flood came the year he died.
persons.life_summary: Son of Enoch and grandfather of Noah. He lived 969 years, the longest recorded lifespan in all of Scripture. His death coincided with the year of the great Flood, possibly a sign of God's long patience.

persons.meaning_connection: He prophesied over his son Noah, saying 'This one shall comfort us in our work and toil,' looking for relief from the curse on the ground.
persons.life_summary: Son of Methuselah and father of Noah. He named Noah with a prophecy: 'This one will comfort us concerning our work and the toil of our hands, because of the ground which the LORD has cursed.' He lived 777 years.

persons.meaning_connection: His father Lamech named him 'rest,' prophesying that he would bring comfort from the curse. Through the ark, he preserved life and received God's covenant.
persons.life_summary: Son of Lamech, a righteous man who walked with God in a corrupt generation. God commanded him to build an ark, through which his family and representatives of all animals were saved from the worldwide Flood. After the Flood, God made a covenant with Noah, signified by the rainbow.

persons.meaning_connection: Though unnamed in the biblical text, she faithfully entered the ark with her husband and was preserved through the Flood to help repopulate the earth.
persons.life_summary: The wife of Noah, unnamed in Scripture. She entered the ark with Noah, their three sons, and their sons' wives, and was preserved through the great Flood.

persons.meaning_connection: Cain named a city after him, dedicating it to his son—a contrast to the Enoch of Seth's line who was dedicated to God.
persons.life_summary: Son of Cain. Cain built a city and named it after his son Enoch. Not to be confused with Enoch son of Jared from the line of Seth.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'name' or 'renown,' and through his line came Abraham, the Israelites, and ultimately the Messiah—the greatest renown.
persons.life_summary: The eldest son of Noah, from whom the Semitic peoples descended. He and his brother Japheth covered their father's nakedness with respect. Noah blessed him saying 'Blessed be the LORD, the God of Shem.' Through his line came Abraham and the chosen people.

persons.meaning_connection: His name may relate to the warm climates where his descendants settled—Africa and the Near East.
persons.life_summary: Son of Noah, father of Cush, Mizraim, Put, and Canaan. After the Flood, he saw his father's nakedness and told his brothers. Noah pronounced a curse upon Ham's son Canaan. Ham's descendants settled in Africa and parts of the Near East.

persons.meaning_connection: Noah prophesied 'May God enlarge Japheth,' and his descendants spread across vast territories in Europe and Asia.
persons.life_summary: Son of Noah, from whom the peoples of Europe and parts of Asia descended. He and Shem respectfully covered their father's nakedness. Noah blessed him saying 'May God enlarge Japheth, and let him dwell in the tents of Shem.'

persons.meaning_connection: Ancestor of the Cimmerians, his descendants spread into areas around the Black Sea and into Europe.
persons.life_summary: Firstborn son of Japheth. His descendants are associated with the Cimmerians, who inhabited regions around the Black Sea. Father of Ashkenaz, Riphath, and Togarmah.

persons.meaning_connection: Associated with northern peoples, his name reappears in Ezekiel's prophecy of Gog and Magog in the end times.
persons.life_summary: Son of Japheth, ancestor of peoples identified with the Scythians and other northern nomadic groups. His name appears in Ezekiel 38-39 in a prophetic context.

persons.meaning_connection: Ancestor of the Medes, who became a powerful empire in ancient Persia and played a role in biblical prophecy.
persons.life_summary: Son of Japheth, ancestor of the Medes. The Medes became a significant empire in the ancient Near East, eventually merging with the Persians under Cyrus the Great.

persons.meaning_connection: His name is the Hebrew word for Greece (Yavan), and his descendants became the Greek and Ionian peoples.
persons.life_summary: Son of Japheth, ancestor of the Greeks and Ionians. The Hebrew name for Greece, 'Yavan,' derives directly from his name. Father of Elishah, Tarshish, Kittim, and Dodanim.

persons.meaning_connection: His descendants are associated with peoples of Asia Minor, mentioned alongside Meshech in Ezekiel's prophecies.
persons.life_summary: Son of Japheth, associated with peoples of Asia Minor, possibly the Tibareni. He is often mentioned alongside Meshech in prophetic texts.

persons.meaning_connection: His descendants settled in Asia Minor and are frequently paired with Tubal in prophetic writings.
persons.life_summary: Son of Japheth, associated with the Mushki peoples of Asia Minor. He is mentioned with Tubal as traders in persons and bronze in Ezekiel.

persons.meaning_connection: His descendants are associated with the Thracians or Tyrrhenians, seafaring peoples of the ancient Mediterranean.
persons.life_summary: Son of Japheth, possibly ancestor of the Thracians or Tyrrhenians. Ancient sources connect him with maritime peoples of the Aegean region.

persons.meaning_connection: His descendants are associated with peoples near the Black Sea, and the name later became associated with Germanic-region populations in medieval tradition.
persons.life_summary: Son of Gomer and grandson of Japheth. Associated with the Scythian peoples near the Black Sea. In Jeremiah, the kingdom of Ashkenaz is summoned against Babylon.

persons.meaning_connection: His descendants are associated with the Paphlagonians or other peoples of Asia Minor.
persons.life_summary: Son of Gomer and grandson of Japheth. Ancient sources associate his descendants with the Paphlagonians in northern Asia Minor.

persons.meaning_connection: His descendants are associated with the Armenians, and they traded horses and mules according to Ezekiel.
persons.life_summary: Son of Gomer and grandson of Japheth. Associated with Armenia and the peoples of the Caucasus region. Ezekiel mentions Beth-Togarmah as traders of horses, war horses, and mules.

persons.meaning_connection: Associated with the Aeolian Greeks or Cyprus, his descendants provided blue and purple dyes to Tyre.
persons.life_summary: Son of Javan and grandson of Japheth. Associated with the Aeolian Greeks or possibly Alashiya (Cyprus). Ezekiel mentions the coastlands of Elishah as a source of blue and purple dyes.

persons.meaning_connection: His name is linked to distant seafaring destinations; 'ships of Tarshish' became a byword for long-distance maritime trade.
persons.life_summary: Son of Javan. Associated with Tartessus in southern Spain or other distant western Mediterranean locations. 'Ships of Tarshish' became synonymous with large merchant vessels in Scripture. Jonah tried to flee to Tarshish.

persons.meaning_connection: Associated with Cyprus (ancient Kition), his descendants became known as a maritime people of the eastern Mediterranean.
persons.life_summary: Son of Javan. Associated with the island of Cyprus, specifically the city of Kition (modern Larnaca). In later usage, the name extended to include peoples of the eastern Mediterranean islands.

persons.meaning_connection: Associated with the peoples of Rhodes and the Aegean islands, possibly the Dardanians near Troy.
persons.life_summary: Son of Javan. Associated with the people of Rhodes (also called Rodanim in 1 Chronicles 1:7) or the Dardanians of the Aegean region.

persons.meaning_connection: His name is associated with the dark-skinned peoples south of Egypt; Cush is the Hebrew name for Ethiopia/Nubia.
persons.life_summary: Firstborn son of Ham. Ancestor of the Cushite peoples, including the Ethiopians and Nubians. Father of Nimrod, the mighty hunter, and of Seba, Havilah, Sabtah, Raamah, and Sabtechah.

persons.meaning_connection: Mizraim is the Hebrew name for Egypt, used throughout the Old Testament to refer to the land and its people.
persons.life_summary: Son of Ham and ancestor of the Egyptians. The Hebrew word for Egypt is 'Mizraim,' a name used hundreds of times in the Old Testament. His descendants settled along the Nile Valley.

persons.meaning_connection: His descendants are associated with the Libyans and North African peoples west of Egypt.
persons.life_summary: Son of Ham, ancestor of the Libyans and other North African peoples west of Egypt. Put's warriors are mentioned as mercenaries serving foreign armies in prophetic texts.

persons.meaning_connection: His descendants occupied the land that God promised to Abraham's seed—a land that bore his name until the Israelite conquest.
persons.life_summary: Son of Ham, upon whom Noah's curse fell. His descendants occupied the land between the Jordan and the Mediterranean, which became known as the land of Canaan. Father of Sidon and Heth and many other Canaanite peoples.

persons.meaning_connection: His descendants are associated with the Sabeans of northeast Africa, mentioned in prophecies of nations honoring God.
persons.life_summary: Firstborn son of Cush. Associated with the Sabeans of the Upper Nile region. Isaiah prophesies that Seba would be given as a ransom for Israel.

persons.meaning_connection: His name evokes sandy terrain, and his descendants are associated with peoples of the Arabian Peninsula.
persons.life_summary: Son of Cush, associated with peoples of the Arabian Peninsula. The land of Havilah is mentioned in Genesis 2 as a place rich in gold, bdellium, and onyx stone.

persons.meaning_connection: His descendants settled in southern Arabia, part of the widespread Cushite diaspora.
persons.life_summary: Son of Cush, associated with ancient peoples of southern Arabia, possibly the Hadramaut region.

persons.meaning_connection: His descendants became South Arabian traders, known for commerce in spices, gold, and precious stones.
persons.life_summary: Son of Cush, ancestor of South Arabian trading peoples. Ezekiel mentions the traders of Raamah dealing in spices, precious stones, and gold.

persons.meaning_connection: The youngest of Cush's sons, his descendants are associated with peoples of eastern Africa or southern Arabia.
persons.life_summary: Son of Cush, the youngest of his five sons besides Nimrod. Associated with peoples of eastern Africa or the southern Arabian coast.

persons.meaning_connection: His name suggests rebellion, and he became the first great empire-builder, establishing Babel and Nineveh in defiance of God's command to spread across the earth.
persons.life_summary: Son of Cush and grandson of Ham, described as 'a mighty hunter before the LORD.' He was the first to become a mighty one on earth. He founded Babel, Erech, Accad, and Calneh in the land of Shinar, and from there went to Assyria and built Nineveh.

persons.meaning_connection: His name relates to fishing, fitting for a coastal city. Sidon became the great Phoenician port city named after him.
persons.life_summary: Firstborn son of Canaan, ancestor of the Sidonians and Phoenicians. The ancient coastal city of Sidon in Lebanon bears his name and was one of the greatest cities of Phoenicia.

persons.meaning_connection: Ancestor of the Hittites, a powerful people whose empire once rivaled Egypt. Abraham purchased the cave of Machpelah from the sons of Heth.
persons.life_summary: Second son of Canaan, ancestor of the Hittites. The sons of Heth are prominent in Genesis as the people from whom Abraham purchased the cave of Machpelah to bury Sarah.

persons.meaning_connection: His descendants founded the ancient kingdom of Elam east of Mesopotamia, which later became part of the Persian Empire.
persons.life_summary: Firstborn son of Shem, ancestor of the Elamites who settled east of Mesopotamia in what is now southwestern Iran. The Elamite kingdom was one of the oldest civilizations.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'strong' or 'a step forward,' and his descendants built the powerful Assyrian Empire, one of the mightiest in the ancient world.
persons.life_summary: Son of Shem, ancestor of the Assyrians. The land of Assyria and its capital cities bore his name. The Assyrian Empire became one of the most powerful empires of the ancient Near East.

persons.meaning_connection: Born two years after the Flood, he continued the chosen line from Shem through which Abraham and the Messiah would come.
persons.life_summary: Son of Shem, born two years after the Flood. He is in the direct messianic line from Shem to Abraham. He lived 438 years and fathered Shelah.

persons.meaning_connection: Ancestor of the Lydians of Asia Minor, a people known for their wealth and invention of coined money.
persons.life_summary: Son of Shem, ancestor of the Lydians who settled in western Asia Minor (modern Turkey). The Lydians became renowned for their wealth under King Croesus.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'highland,' fitting for the elevated region of Syria where his descendants settled. The Aramaic language bears his name.
persons.life_summary: Son of Shem, ancestor of the Arameans who inhabited Syria and Mesopotamia. The Aramaic language, which became the common tongue of the ancient Near East, is named after him. Abraham's family came from Aram-Naharaim.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'sent forth,' continuing the messianic line that was sent through the generations from Shem to Abraham.
persons.life_summary: Son of Arphaxad and grandson of Shem, in the direct line from Shem to Abraham. He lived 433 years and fathered Eber.

persons.name_meaning: one who crosses over, region beyond
persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'to cross over,' and from his name comes the word 'Hebrew'—those who crossed over, both geographically and spiritually.
persons.life_summary: Son of Shelah, ancestor from whom the term 'Hebrew' derives. He lived 464 years, the longest post-Flood lifespan in Shem's line. He named his son Peleg because 'in his days the earth was divided.'

persons.meaning_connection: Named 'division' because in his days the earth was divided—likely referring to the division of peoples at the Tower of Babel.
persons.life_summary: Son of Eber, named Peleg ('division') because in his days the earth was divided, most likely referring to the confusion of languages at Babel and the scattering of nations. He lived 239 years.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'friend' or 'shepherd,' continuing the faithful line from Shem toward Abraham.
persons.life_summary: Son of Peleg, in the direct line from Shem to Abraham. He lived 239 years and fathered Serug.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'branch,' a link in the genealogical branch that would lead to Abraham and eventually to the promised Messiah.
persons.life_summary: Son of Reu, in the direct line from Shem to Abraham. He lived 230 years and fathered Nahor.

persons.meaning_connection: Grandfather of Abraham, his name is also the name of the city in Mesopotamia where Abraham's family settled.
persons.life_summary: Son of Serug and grandfather of Abraham. He lived 148 years and fathered Terah. The city of Nahor in Mesopotamia may have been named after him.

persons.meaning_connection: He began the journey from Ur toward Canaan but stopped and settled in Haran—his name meaning 'delay' reflects his incomplete journey.
persons.life_summary: Son of Nahor and father of Abraham, Nahor, and Haran. He took his family from Ur of the Chaldeans intending to go to Canaan but settled in Haran, where he died at age 205. Joshua 24:2 says he served other gods beyond the River.

persons.meaning_connection: God changed his name from Abram ('exalted father') to Abraham ('father of a multitude') as a sign of the covenant promise that he would be the father of many nations.
persons.life_summary: Called by God to leave Ur and go to a land God would show him. God made a covenant with him, promising land, descendants as numerous as the stars, and that all nations would be blessed through his seed. He is the father of faith and the patriarch of the Israelites through Isaac.

persons.meaning_connection: God changed her name from Sarai to Sarah ('princess'), declaring she would be a mother of nations and that kings of peoples would come from her.
persons.life_summary: Wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac, born barren but given a son by God's promise at age 90. She is honored as a woman of faith. She died at 127 years in Hebron, and Abraham purchased the cave of Machpelah to bury her.
persons.scripture_refs: Genesis 17:15-16, Genesis 18:10-14, Genesis 21:1-3, Genesis 23:1-2, Hebrews 11:11, 1 Peter 3:6

persons.meaning_connection: Her name means 'flight' or 'stranger,' and she fled from Sarah into the wilderness, where the Angel of the LORD found her and promised her descendants.
persons.life_summary: Egyptian servant of Sarah, given to Abraham as a wife. She bore Ishmael, Abraham's first son. Twice she was sent into the wilderness—once fleeing and once expelled—but God heard her cries and promised to make Ishmael a great nation.

persons.meaning_connection: Named by the Angel of the LORD because 'God has heard your affliction'—Hagar's cry in the wilderness was answered.
persons.life_summary: Firstborn son of Abraham by Hagar. God promised he would become a great nation and father twelve princes. He lived 137 years. He and Isaac together buried Abraham. He is regarded as the ancestor of many Arab peoples.

persons.meaning_connection: Both Abraham and Sarah laughed when God promised them a son in their old age; God named him 'laughter' as a testimony to His faithfulness beyond human expectation.
persons.life_summary: The son of promise, born to Abraham and Sarah in their old age. He was nearly sacrificed on Mount Moriah in a test of Abraham's faith, foreshadowing God's provision. He married Rebekah and fathered Esau and Jacob. He lived 180 years.
persons.scripture_refs: Genesis 21:1-7, Genesis 22:1-14, Genesis 24, Genesis 25:19-26, Genesis 35:28-29, Hebrews 11:17-20

persons.meaning_connection: Her beauty and character captivated Abraham's servant, and she was chosen by God's providence as the wife for Isaac to continue the covenant line.
persons.life_summary: Daughter of Bethuel and wife of Isaac. She was barren for twenty years until Isaac prayed for her. God told her two nations were in her womb and the older would serve the younger. She favored Jacob and helped him obtain Isaac's blessing.

persons.meaning_connection: He came out red and hairy at birth, and his impulsive nature led him to sell his birthright for a bowl of stew—choosing the immediate over the eternal.
persons.life_summary: Firstborn son of Isaac and Rebekah, twin brother of Jacob. He sold his birthright for a bowl of stew and lost his father's blessing to Jacob. He became the father of the Edomites and settled in the hill country of Seir.

persons.meaning_connection: He grasped Esau's heel at birth and later supplanted him for both birthright and blessing. God renamed him Israel ('he who strives with God'), transforming the supplanter into a prince.
persons.life_summary: Son of Isaac and Rebekah, twin of Esau. He obtained the birthright and blessing, fled to Haran, worked for Laban, and married Leah and Rachel. He wrestled with God at Peniel and was renamed Israel. Father of twelve sons who became the twelve tribes of Israel. He lived 147 years and died in Egypt.
persons.scripture_refs: Genesis 25:26, Genesis 27-33, Genesis 32:28, Genesis 35:22-26, Genesis 49, Hebrews 11:21

persons.meaning_connection: Though 'weary' in name and unloved by Jacob, God saw her affliction and blessed her with many sons, including Judah, through whom the Messiah would come.
persons.life_summary: Daughter of Laban and first wife of Jacob, given to him by deception. Though Jacob loved Rachel more, God opened Leah's womb and she bore six sons and a daughter. She is buried in the cave of Machpelah with Jacob.

persons.meaning_connection: A shepherdess by occupation (fitting her name), she was deeply loved by Jacob. Her death in childbirth giving birth to Benjamin made her a symbol of maternal sacrifice.
persons.life_summary: Daughter of Laban and beloved wife of Jacob, for whom he worked fourteen years. She was barren for many years before God opened her womb. She bore Joseph and died giving birth to Benjamin on the road to Bethlehem, where she was buried.

persons.meaning_connection: Leah named him 'behold, a son,' saying 'the LORD has looked upon my affliction; now my husband will love me.'
persons.life_summary: Firstborn son of Jacob and Leah. He lost his birthright because he defiled his father's bed. He tried to save Joseph from his brothers and offered his own sons as surety for Benjamin. Jacob said he was 'unstable as water.'

persons.meaning_connection: Leah named him Simeon, saying 'Because the LORD has heard that I am unloved, He has given me this son also.'
persons.life_summary: Second son of Jacob and Leah. He and Levi massacred the men of Shechem to avenge their sister Dinah. Jacob cursed their anger, and Simeon's tribe was later absorbed into Judah's territory.

persons.meaning_connection: Leah said 'Now this time my husband will become attached to me, because I have borne him three sons.' His descendants became attached to God's service as the priestly tribe.
persons.life_summary: Third son of Jacob and Leah. He and Simeon took violent revenge on Shechem. Despite Jacob's rebuke, his descendants were chosen by God as the priestly tribe. Moses and Aaron descended from Levi.

persons.meaning_connection: Leah said 'This time I will praise the LORD,' and from the tribe of praise came the royal line of David and the Messiah—the Lion of the tribe of Judah.
persons.life_summary: Fourth son of Jacob and Leah. He proposed selling Joseph rather than killing him and later offered himself as surety for Benjamin. Jacob prophesied 'The scepter shall not depart from Judah.' From his line came King David and the Messiah.

persons.meaning_connection: Rachel named him Dan, saying 'God has judged me and heard my voice.' Jacob prophesied 'Dan shall judge his people.'
persons.life_summary: Son of Jacob by Bilhah, Rachel's servant. Jacob blessed him saying 'Dan shall judge his people as one of the tribes of Israel.' His tribe settled in the northern part of the land, and Samson was from the tribe of Dan.

persons.meaning_connection: Rachel said 'With mighty wrestlings I have wrestled with my sister, and I have prevailed,' reflecting the rivalry between the sisters.
persons.life_summary: Son of Jacob by Bilhah, Rachel's servant. Jacob blessed him saying 'Naphtali is a doe set free that bears beautiful fawns,' or in another reading, 'who gives beautiful words.' His tribe settled in the region of Galilee.

persons.meaning_connection: Leah said 'Good fortune has come!' or 'A troop comes,' and Jacob prophesied that a troop would press upon Gad but he would press upon their heel.
persons.life_summary: Son of Jacob by Zilpah, Leah's servant. Jacob prophesied 'Gad, a troop shall tramp upon him, but he shall triumph at last.' His tribe became known as fierce warriors and settled east of the Jordan.

persons.meaning_connection: Leah said 'Happy am I, for the daughters will call me blessed,' and his tribe was indeed blessed with rich food and fertile land.
persons.life_summary: Son of Jacob by Zilpah, Leah's servant. Jacob blessed him saying 'Asher's food shall be rich, and he shall yield royal delicacies.' His tribe inherited fertile coastal land. The prophetess Anna in the New Testament was from the tribe of Asher.

persons.meaning_connection: Leah said 'God has given me my wages (reward),' seeing Issachar as God's recompense for giving her servant to Jacob.
persons.life_summary: Fifth son of Jacob and Leah. Jacob described him as 'a strong donkey lying down between the sheepfolds,' a tribe that saw the good land and bowed to labor. His descendants were known for understanding the times.

persons.meaning_connection: Leah said 'God has endowed me with a good endowment; now my husband will dwell with me,' hoping for honor through her sixth son.
persons.life_summary: Sixth son of Jacob and Leah. Jacob prophesied 'Zebulun shall dwell by the haven of the sea; he shall become a haven for ships, and his border shall adjoin Sidon.' His tribe settled in the region of Galilee.

persons.name_meaning: he will add, may God add
persons.meaning_connection: Rachel named him Joseph, saying 'The LORD shall add to me another son.' God kept adding to Joseph—from pit to prison to palace.
persons.life_summary: Firstborn son of Jacob and Rachel, beloved by his father. His brothers sold him into slavery in Egypt, where he rose from prisoner to second-in-command under Pharaoh. He saved his family and Egypt from famine, and his two sons Ephraim and Manasseh became tribes of Israel. He lived 110 years.

persons.meaning_connection: His dying mother Rachel named him Ben-oni ('son of my sorrow'), but Jacob renamed him Benjamin ('son of the right hand')—turning grief into honor.
persons.life_summary: Youngest son of Jacob and Rachel. His mother died giving birth to him. He was especially loved by Jacob after Joseph's disappearance. Jacob prophesied 'Benjamin is a ravenous wolf.' The apostle Paul and King Saul were from the tribe of Benjamin.

persons.meaning_connection: Joseph named him Ephraim saying 'God has made me fruitful in the land of my affliction.' Jacob placed his right hand on Ephraim, the younger, blessing him above Manasseh.
persons.life_summary: Second son of Joseph, born in Egypt. Though younger than Manasseh, Jacob crossed his hands and placed the greater blessing on Ephraim, saying 'his descendants shall become a multitude of nations.' His tribe became the dominant tribe of the northern kingdom.

persons.meaning_connection: Joseph named him Manasseh saying 'God has made me forget all my hardship and all my father's house'—God's grace caused the pain of the past to fade.
persons.life_summary: Firstborn son of Joseph, born in Egypt. Though the firstborn, he received the lesser blessing when Jacob crossed his hands. His tribe received a large territory on both sides of the Jordan River.

persons.meaning_connection: He broke through before his twin brother Zerah at birth, and through his line the royal Messianic lineage broke through from Judah to David.
persons.life_summary: Son of Judah and Tamar, twin brother of Zerah. At birth he broke through before his brother, hence his name. He is in the direct Messianic line from Judah to David to the Messiah.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'enclosed,' and he carried the enclosed promise of God's covenant—the Messianic line passing through him from Perez to Ram.
persons.life_summary: Son of Perez and grandson of Judah, in the direct Messianic line. He was among those who went down to Egypt with Jacob. He is listed in the genealogies of both Ruth and Matthew as an ancestor of David.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'exalted,' and he carried the exalted promise—the line that would produce King David and ultimately the Messiah.
persons.life_summary: Son of Hezron, in the direct Messianic line from Judah to David. He is listed in the genealogies of Ruth and Matthew.

persons.name_meaning: my people are noble, my kinsman is generous
persons.meaning_connection: His name speaks of the nobility of his people, and through his line came the noblest king of Israel—David.
persons.life_summary: Son of Ram, in the direct Messianic line. His daughter Elisheba married Aaron the high priest, connecting the royal and priestly lines. Father of Nahshon, the leader of the tribe of Judah in the wilderness.

persons.meaning_connection: Despite his name's literal meaning, he was a leader of Judah in the wilderness. Jewish tradition says he was the first to step into the Red Sea in faith.
persons.life_summary: Son of Amminadab and leader (nasi) of the tribe of Judah during the wilderness wandering. He was the first tribal leader to bring his offering at the dedication of the tabernacle. Brother-in-law to Aaron the high priest.

persons.meaning_connection: According to tradition, he married Rahab of Jericho, showing God's grace in grafting a Canaanite woman of faith into the Messianic line.
persons.life_summary: Son of Nahshon, in the direct Messianic line. According to Matthew's genealogy, he married Rahab (of Jericho) and fathered Boaz. His inclusion of Rahab shows God's grace extended beyond Israel.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'in him is strength,' and he showed strength of character as the kinsman-redeemer who married Ruth, foreshadowing the ultimate Redeemer.
persons.life_summary: Son of Salmon, a wealthy landowner in Bethlehem. He acted as kinsman-redeemer for Ruth the Moabitess, marrying her and redeeming the family inheritance. His role as redeemer foreshadows the Messiah. Father of Obed, grandfather of Jesse, great-grandfather of David.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'servant,' and he served as a vital link in the Messianic line—the son of Boaz and Ruth, grandfather of King David.
persons.life_summary: Son of Boaz and Ruth, father of Jesse, grandfather of King David. The women of Bethlehem said to Naomi, 'A son has been born to Naomi,' and they named him Obed.

persons.meaning_connection: The 'root of Jesse' became a Messianic title—Isaiah prophesied that a shoot would come from the stump of Jesse, and a branch from his roots would bear fruit.
persons.life_summary: Son of Obed and father of King David. A Bethlehemite and shepherd, he had eight sons, of whom David was the youngest. The prophet Samuel came to his house to anoint the future king. 'The root of Jesse' became a prophetic symbol of the Messiah.

persons.meaning_connection: The beloved king after God's own heart, through whom God established an eternal covenant—that from David's throne the Messiah would reign forever.
persons.life_summary: The youngest son of Jesse, anointed king by Samuel while still a shepherd boy. He defeated Goliath, became king of all Israel, conquered Jerusalem, and established the united kingdom. God made an everlasting covenant with him that his throne would endure forever. He is called a man after God's own heart.
persons.scripture_refs: 1 Samuel 16:1-13, 1 Samuel 17, 2 Samuel 5:1-5, 2 Samuel 7:12-16, Acts 13:22, Matthew 1:1

persons.meaning_connection: Her name means 'daughter of the oath,' and through her son Solomon, God's covenant oath to David was carried forward.
persons.life_summary: Wife of Uriah the Hittite, then wife of King David. Mother of Solomon. She advocated for Solomon's succession to the throne and became queen mother.

persons.meaning_connection: His name comes from 'shalom' (peace). God granted peace during his reign so he could build the Temple, and he foreshadowed the Prince of Peace.
persons.life_summary: Son of David and Bathsheba, the wisest king of Israel. He built the first Temple in Jerusalem. God gave him unparalleled wisdom, wealth, and honor. His reign was the golden age of Israel, but his foreign wives turned his heart to other gods in his old age.

persons.meaning_connection: Ironically, his name means 'he enlarges the people,' but his harsh rule caused the kingdom to split—ten tribes broke away under Jeroboam.
persons.life_summary: Son of Solomon, first king of the divided southern kingdom of Judah. When he refused the people's plea for lighter burdens, ten tribes revolted, splitting the united kingdom. He reigned 17 years in Jerusalem.

persons.meaning_connection: His name declares 'my father is the LORD,' and despite his flawed reign he trusted God in battle against Jeroboam and was victorious.
persons.life_summary: Son of Rehoboam, king of Judah. He reigned three years and waged war against Jeroboam of Israel. He rebuked the northern kingdom for abandoning the LORD and won a decisive battle through faith in God.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'healer,' and he healed the land of idolatry, removing pagan altars and restoring worship of the LORD.
persons.life_summary: Son of Abijah, a righteous king of Judah who reigned 41 years. He removed the foreign altars and high places, commanded Judah to seek the LORD, and defeated a massive Ethiopian army by trusting God. In his later years he relied on foreign alliances rather than God.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'the LORD has judged,' and he was known for establishing judges throughout the land and seeking God's judgment in all matters.
persons.life_summary: Son of Asa, a righteous king of Judah who reigned 25 years. He sent teachers throughout the land to instruct the people in God's law, appointed judges, and trusted God when a vast army invaded—winning victory through worship and prayer.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'the LORD is exalted,' yet he walked in the ways of the kings of Israel and did evil, showing how a name can contrast with a life.
persons.life_summary: Son of Jehoshaphat, king of Judah. He married Athaliah, daughter of Ahab, and walked in the wicked ways of the kings of Israel. He killed his brothers and led Judah into idolatry. He died of a terrible disease as prophesied by Elijah.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'my strength is the LORD,' and he prospered as long as he sought God—but when pride replaced dependence, God struck him with leprosy.
persons.life_summary: Also called Azariah, king of Judah who reigned 52 years. He strengthened Judah militarily and agriculturally. He sought God and prospered, but when he became proud and entered the Temple to burn incense—a priestly duty—God struck him with leprosy. Note: Matthew's genealogy skips three kings (Ahaziah, Joash, Amaziah) between Joram and Uzziah.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'the LORD is perfect,' and he was one of the more faithful kings—he did what was right but did not enter the Temple as his father had presumptuously done.
persons.life_summary: Son of Uzziah, king of Judah who reigned 16 years. He did what was right in the eyes of the LORD, rebuilt the upper gate of the Temple, and built cities and fortresses. However, the people still followed corrupt practices.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'he has grasped,' yet he grasped after false gods rather than the LORD, even sacrificing his own children to Molech.
persons.life_summary: Son of Jotham, one of the most wicked kings of Judah. He practiced child sacrifice, worshipped Baal, and shut the doors of the Temple. When threatened by Syria and Israel, he refused Isaiah's sign from God and instead sought help from Assyria, making Judah a vassal state.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'the LORD is my strength,' and he proved it by trusting God against the Assyrian siege when all seemed lost—and God delivered Jerusalem.
persons.life_summary: Son of Ahaz, one of the greatest kings of Judah. He reopened and cleansed the Temple, destroyed the high places, and trusted God when Sennacherib's Assyrian army besieged Jerusalem—God sent an angel who struck down 185,000 soldiers. God also extended his life by 15 years after his prayer.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'causing to forget,' and he caused Israel to forget God's ways through extreme wickedness—yet after captivity in Babylon, he repented and God restored him.
persons.life_summary: Son of Hezekiah, the longest-reigning and most wicked king of Judah (55 years). He rebuilt the high places, worshipped Baal and the host of heaven, practiced sorcery, and shed much innocent blood. After being captured by Assyria, he repented in prison, and God restored him to Jerusalem.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'faithful,' but he was unfaithful to God—he followed his father Manasseh's early wickedness but not his later repentance.
persons.life_summary: Son of Manasseh, king of Judah who reigned only two years. He did evil in the sight of the LORD, worshipping the idols his father had served. Unlike Manasseh, he did not humble himself before God. His own servants conspired against him and assassinated him.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'the LORD heals,' and he healed Judah's spiritual corruption through the greatest reformation in the nation's history.
persons.life_summary: Son of Amon, the last righteous king of Judah. At age eight he became king; at sixteen he began seeking God; at twenty he purged idolatry from the land. When the Book of the Law was found in the Temple, he tore his robes in grief and led the greatest national reformation. He was killed in battle at Megiddo against Pharaoh Necho.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'the LORD will establish,' and though God cursed his direct line from the throne, the Messianic promise was ultimately established through his descendants.
persons.life_summary: Also called Jehoiachin and Coniah, son of Jehoiakim (grandson of Josiah). He was king of Judah for only three months before Nebuchadnezzar carried him captive to Babylon in 597 BC. Jeremiah pronounced a curse that none of his descendants would sit on David's throne. After 37 years in prison, the king of Babylon released him and gave him a seat of honor.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'I have asked of God'—born in exile, his very name was a prayer for restoration and deliverance.
persons.life_summary: Son of Jeconiah, born during the Babylonian captivity. His name reflects the exiles' desperate plea to God for deliverance. Father of Zerubbabel, who would lead the first return from exile.

persons.name_meaning: seed of Babylon / born in Babylon
persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'seed of Babylon'—born in captivity, yet chosen by God to lead the return and rebuild the Temple, a type of the Messiah restoring what was destroyed.
persons.life_summary: Son of Shealtiel, governor of Judah who led the first group of exiles back from Babylon to Jerusalem. He laid the foundation of the Second Temple and completed its rebuilding despite fierce opposition. God declared him His 'signet ring,' reversing the curse on Jeconiah's line.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'my father is majesty,' honoring the royal Davidic line even during the post-exilic period when there was no king on the throne.
persons.life_summary: Son of Zerubbabel, a descendant of David in the post-exilic period. He carried the Messianic line forward during the centuries between the return from Babylon and the birth of Christ. Known only from Matthew's genealogy.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'God raises up'—a declaration of faith that God would raise up a deliverer from David's line even in the silent generations.
persons.life_summary: Son of Abiud, a post-exilic descendant in the royal line of David. He lived during the intertestamental period, faithfully carrying the Messianic lineage. Known only from Matthew's genealogy.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'helper,' echoing the promise that from David's line God would send the ultimate Helper for His people.
persons.life_summary: Son of Eliakim, a post-exilic descendant in the line of David. He lived during the period of Persian or Greek rule over Judea. Known only from Matthew's genealogy.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'righteous,' pointing toward the Righteous One who would come from his lineage—the Messiah, called 'The LORD Our Righteousness.'
persons.life_summary: Son of Azor, a post-exilic descendant in the royal line of David. His name, meaning 'righteous,' kept alive the hope for the Righteous Branch prophesied by Jeremiah. Known only from Matthew's genealogy.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'the LORD will establish,' a continued declaration that God's promise to David would be fulfilled.
persons.life_summary: Son of Zadok, a post-exilic descendant in the line of David. He lived during the Hellenistic period as the Messianic promise drew closer to fulfillment. Known only from Matthew's genealogy.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'God is my praise,' reflecting faithful worship passed down through generations awaiting the promised Messiah.
persons.life_summary: Son of Achim, a post-exilic descendant in the royal line of David. He lived during the late Hellenistic or early Roman period. Known only from Matthew's genealogy.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'God has helped,' a testimony that through centuries of foreign rule, God continued to help and preserve the Messianic line.
persons.life_summary: Son of Eliud, a descendant of David living in the Roman period. The Messianic promise was nearing fulfillment. Known only from Matthew's genealogy.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'gift,' and his grandson Joseph would receive the greatest gift—the honor of raising the Messiah.
persons.life_summary: Son of Eleazar, grandfather of Joseph the husband of Mary. He lived in the generation just before the birth of Christ. Known only from Matthew's genealogy.

persons.meaning_connection: Sharing the name of the patriarch Jacob/Israel, he was the last link before the legal father of the Messiah, connecting the promise made to Israel with its fulfillment.
persons.life_summary: Son of Matthan and father of Joseph, the husband of Mary. The final generation before the birth of Jesus the Messiah. Known only from Matthew's genealogy.

persons.meaning_connection: Like the patriarch Joseph, his name means 'he will add.' God added to him the immeasurable honor of being the legal father and protector of the Messiah.
persons.life_summary: Son of Jacob, a righteous carpenter from Nazareth descended from King David. He was betrothed to Mary when an angel revealed she would bear the Messiah by the Holy Spirit. He obeyed God, taking Mary as his wife and becoming the legal father of Jesus. He protected the family by fleeing to Egypt from Herod, and later settled in Nazareth.

persons.meaning_connection: From the Hebrew Miriam, her name carries echoes of both bitterness and exaltation—she would bear the sorrow of watching her son crucified and the exaltation of being the mother of the Messiah.
persons.life_summary: A young virgin from Nazareth, betrothed to Joseph. The angel Gabriel announced that she would conceive by the Holy Spirit and bear the Son of God. She responded in faith: 'Be it unto me according to thy word.' She treasured all things in her heart and stood at the foot of the cross.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'the LORD saves,' given by the angel before His birth: 'thou shalt call his name Jesus, for he shall save his people from their sins.' He is the fulfillment of every promise in the lineage.
persons.life_summary: The Son of God, born of the virgin Mary in Bethlehem, the promised Messiah descended from Abraham and David. He lived a sinless life, taught with authority, performed miracles, and fulfilled over 300 Old Testament prophecies. He was crucified for the sins of the world, buried, and rose again on the third day. He is the Lion of the Tribe of Judah, the Root and Offspring of David, the Bright and Morning Star.
persons.scripture_refs: Matthew 1:1, Matthew 1:16, Luke 2:1-20, John 1:1-14, John 3:16, Revelation 5:5, Revelation 22:16

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'he gave,' and through his line God gave the biological lineage of the Messiah through Mary, as recorded in Luke's genealogy.
persons.life_summary: Son of David and Bathsheba, born in Jerusalem. Unlike his brother Solomon who inherited the throne, Nathan's line carried the biological descent to the Messiah through Mary. His line is recorded in Luke 3.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'gift of God,' continuing the theme of divine giving through Nathan's line.
persons.life_summary: Son of Nathan, grandson of David. A link in the Messianic line recorded in Luke's genealogy, tracing the biological descent to Jesus through Mary.

persons.meaning_connection: His name suggests being 'numbered' or 'appointed'—appointed by God as a link in the chain leading to the Messiah.
persons.life_summary: Son of Mattatha, a descendant of David through Nathan. Known only from Luke's genealogy of Jesus.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'fullness,' and through his line the fullness of time would come when God sent His Son.
persons.life_summary: Son of Menna, a descendant of David through Nathan. Known only from Luke's genealogy of Jesus.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'God raises up,' a recurring testimony of faith in the Messianic line.
persons.life_summary: Son of Melea, a descendant of David through Nathan. Not to be confused with Eliakim son of Abiud in Matthew's genealogy. Known only from Luke's genealogy.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'God is gracious,' a testament to divine grace sustaining the Messianic line through the centuries.
persons.life_summary: Son of Eliakim, a descendant of David through Nathan. Known only from Luke's genealogy of Jesus.

persons.meaning_connection: A common Hebrew name meaning 'he will add,' carried by multiple figures in the Messianic line.
persons.life_summary: Son of Jonam, a descendant of David through Nathan. Known only from Luke's genealogy of Jesus.

persons.meaning_connection: Sharing the name of the patriarch, meaning 'praise'—the praise of God echoed through the generations.
persons.life_summary: Son of Joseph, a descendant of David through Nathan. Known only from Luke's genealogy of Jesus.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'heard'—God heard the prayers of His people and preserved the line of promise.
persons.life_summary: Son of Judah, a descendant of David through Nathan. Known only from Luke's genealogy of Jesus.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'joined,' and he was joined to the purpose of God in carrying the Messianic line.
persons.life_summary: Son of Simeon, a descendant of David through Nathan. Known only from Luke's genealogy of Jesus.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'gift of God,' a recurring declaration that each generation was a gift preserving the promise.
persons.life_summary: Son of Levi, a descendant of David through Nathan. Known only from Luke's genealogy of Jesus.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'the LORD is exalted,' a declaration of faith preserved through the generations.
persons.life_summary: Son of Matthat, a descendant of David through Nathan. Known only from Luke's genealogy of Jesus.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'God is my help,' echoing the dependence on God that sustained the Messianic line.
persons.life_summary: Son of Jorim, a descendant of David through Nathan. Known only from Luke's genealogy of Jesus.

persons.meaning_connection: Sharing the same Hebrew name as Jesus (Yeshua), meaning 'the LORD saves'—a prophetic echo of the Savior to come from this very line.
persons.life_summary: Son of Eliezer, a descendant of David through Nathan. His name is the same as Jesus in Hebrew. Known only from Luke's genealogy.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'watchful'—the line remained watchful for the coming of the promised Redeemer.
persons.life_summary: Son of Joshua, a descendant of David through Nathan. Not to be confused with Er son of Judah. Known only from Luke's genealogy.

persons.name_meaning: God is measure / God of measure
persons.meaning_connection: His name suggests 'God is the measure,' declaring God's sovereignty in measuring out the generations to the Messiah.
persons.life_summary: Son of Er, a descendant of David through Nathan. Known only from Luke's genealogy of Jesus.

persons.meaning_connection: His name relates to 'allotment,' reflecting God's allotted plan unfolding through each generation.
persons.life_summary: Son of Elmadam, a descendant of David through Nathan. Known only from Luke's genealogy of Jesus.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'my witness,' testifying to God's faithfulness across the generations.
persons.life_summary: Son of Cosam, a descendant of David through Nathan. Known only from Luke's genealogy of Jesus.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'my king,' a quiet declaration that the King of Kings would come from this line.
persons.life_summary: Son of Addi, a descendant of David through Nathan. Known only from Luke's genealogy of Jesus.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'my lamp,' and through his line the Light of the World would come.
persons.life_summary: Son of Melchi, a descendant of David through Nathan. Father of Shealtiel in Luke's genealogy. Some scholars see Neri as the point where Nathan's line merged with Solomon's line through Shealtiel, possibly through marriage after Jeconiah's curse.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'head' or 'chief,' possibly a title (Aramaic 'resha') given to descendants of the royal line in exile.
persons.life_summary: Son of Zerubbabel according to Luke's genealogy. After the lines of Matthew and Luke converge at Zerubbabel, they diverge again—Matthew follows Abiud, while Luke follows Rhesa. This traces Mary's biological line.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'God is gracious,' echoing the ongoing grace that sustained the line of promise.
persons.life_summary: Son of Rhesa, a post-exilic descendant of David through the line traced in Luke's genealogy.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'praised,' continuing the thread of praise to God through the lineage.
persons.life_summary: Son of Joanan, a post-exilic descendant of David. Known only from Luke's genealogy of Jesus.

persons.meaning_connection: A variant of Joseph, meaning 'he will add'—God kept adding to the generations of promise.
persons.life_summary: Son of Joda, a post-exilic descendant of David. Known only from Luke's genealogy of Jesus.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'heard'—God heard and continued to preserve the seed of promise.
persons.life_summary: Son of Josech, a post-exilic descendant of David. Known only from Luke's genealogy of Jesus.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'gift of the LORD,' declaring that each generation was a divine gift moving toward fulfillment.
persons.life_summary: Son of Semein, a post-exilic descendant of David. Known only from Luke's genealogy of Jesus.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'small,' yet from small and humble origins God brings great things—the Messiah would come from a humble family.
persons.life_summary: Son of Mattathias, a descendant of David. Known only from Luke's genealogy of Jesus.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'brightness,' foreshadowing the Bright and Morning Star who would come from his line.
persons.life_summary: Son of Maath, a descendant of David. Known only from Luke's genealogy of Jesus.

persons.meaning_connection: His name suggests being 'reserved by God'—set apart in the divine plan.
persons.life_summary: Son of Naggai, a descendant of David. Known only from Luke's genealogy of Jesus.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'comfort'—the Messiah from his line would be called the Consolation of Israel.
persons.life_summary: Son of Esli, a descendant of David. Known only from Luke's genealogy of Jesus.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'burden-bearer,' foreshadowing Christ who would bear the burdens and sins of the world.
persons.life_summary: Son of Nahum, a descendant of David. Not to be confused with the prophet Amos. Known only from Luke's genealogy of Jesus.

persons.meaning_connection: Another 'gift of the LORD' in the genealogy—God's gifts continued through each generation.
persons.life_summary: Son of Amos, a descendant of David. Known only from Luke's genealogy of Jesus.

persons.meaning_connection: Meaning 'he will add,' another Joseph in the line—God added generation upon generation toward fulfillment.
persons.life_summary: Son of Mattathias, a descendant of David. Known only from Luke's genealogy of Jesus.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'God answers'—the answer to centuries of Messianic longing drew nearer with each generation.
persons.life_summary: Son of Joseph, a descendant of David. Known only from Luke's genealogy of Jesus.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'my king'—a declaration of loyalty to God as King, and anticipation of the coming King from David's line.
persons.life_summary: Son of Jannai, a descendant of David. Not to be confused with Melchi son of Addi earlier in the genealogy. Known only from Luke's genealogy.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'joined'—in him the generations were joined ever closer to the fulfillment of the promise.
persons.life_summary: Son of Melchi, a descendant of David. Known only from Luke's genealogy of Jesus.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'gift of God'—the greatest Gift was now just two generations away.
persons.life_summary: Son of Levi, grandfather of Mary the mother of Jesus. Known only from Luke's genealogy.

persons.meaning_connection: His name means 'ascending' or 'exalted.' He is widely understood to be the father of Mary, making him the maternal grandfather of Jesus.
persons.life_summary: Son of Matthat and father of Mary the mother of Jesus. Luke 3:23 says Jesus was 'the son (as was supposed) of Joseph, the son of Heli'—many scholars understand Heli as Mary's father, with Joseph being his son-in-law. Through Heli, Jesus received His biological descent from David through Nathan's line.

modern_connections.notes: Javan (Yavan) is the Hebrew word for Greece used throughout the Old Testament, including in Daniel and Zechariah
Mizraim is the Hebrew name for Egypt used throughout the Old Testament, appearing hundreds of times
Cush is the biblical name for the region south of Egypt, encompassing ancient Nubia and Ethiopia
Canaan's descendants occupied the land promised to Abraham, including the Phoenician coastal cities
Asshur is both the ancestor and the name of the Assyrian homeland and its chief deity
The Elamite kingdom was one of the earliest civilizations, with its capital at Susa, later absorbed into the Persian Empire
The Medes established a powerful empire that later merged with Persia under Cyrus the Great

modern_connections.region: Black Sea region, later migrating into Asia Minor and Europe
modern_connections.source: Genesis 10:2-3; Josephus, Antiquities I.6; Assyrian records
modern_connections.notes: The Cimmerians were a nomadic people from the Black Sea steppes mentioned in Assyrian annals

modern_connections.region: Central Asian steppes, north of the Black Sea and Caucasus
modern_connections.notes: Josephus explicitly identifies Magog with the Scythians, nomadic warriors of the Eurasian steppe
Tarshish is associated with Tartessus in southern Spain, representing the farthest western trading destination known to the ancient Hebrews
Kittim derives from Kition (modern Larnaca), a major ancient city on Cyprus; the term later broadened to include other Mediterranean islanders
Sidon was one of the greatest Phoenician city-states, a center of maritime trade and purple dye production

modern_connections.region: Asia Minor (central Turkey) and northern Levant
modern_connections.notes: The Hittites built one of the great empires of the ancient Near East with their capital at Hattusa in modern Turkey
Nimrod was the first empire-builder, founding Babel, Erech, Accad, and Calneh in Shinar, and Nineveh, Rehoboth-Ir, Calah, and Resen in Assyria
The Arameans inhabited Syria and Mesopotamia; their language Aramaic became the lingua franca of the ancient Near East and was spoken by the Israelites in exile
The Lydians of western Turkey were known for their wealth and are credited with inventing coined money under King Croesus
Put's descendants settled in North Africa west of Egypt, in the region known to the Greeks as Libya

modern_connections.region: Black Sea region, later associated with central/eastern Europe
modern_connections.notes: Originally associated with Scythian peoples near the Black Sea; in medieval tradition the name became associated with Germanic-region populations

modern_connections.region: Armenia, eastern Asia Minor, Caucasus region
modern_connections.source: Genesis 10:3; Ezekiel 27:14, Ezekiel 38:6; Josephus, Antiquities I.6
modern_connections.notes: Armenian tradition claims descent from Togarmah (called Torgom). Ezekiel identifies Beth-Togarmah as traders of horses

modern_connections.region: Greek islands, possibly Cyprus or Aeolia
modern_connections.notes: Associated with the Aeolian Greeks or Alashiya (an ancient name for Cyprus); known for exporting blue and purple textiles
Ishmael is regarded as the ancestor of many Arab peoples; God promised he would father twelve princes and become a great nation
Esau, also called Edom ('red'), settled in the hill country of Seir; the Edomites were persistent rivals of Israel throughout the Old Testament
Distinguished from Sheba; Seba is associated with the African Sabeans in the region of modern Sudan or Eritrea
The land of Havilah is described as rich in gold, bdellium, and onyx; it is associated with regions of the Arabian Peninsula
Raamah's descendants were known as traders in spices, precious stones, and gold, centered in the southern Arabian Peninsula
